34.reason: The condition only treats space as a word delimiter. According to the problem, a "word" is a sequence of letters. Any non-letter character (like digits or punctuation) should act as a delimiter. This causes words adjacent to numbers or punctuation (e.g., "since1972") to be merged incorrectly or handled improperly.

36.reason: [This loop condition assumes that all entries up to k are non-empty, but uninitialized entries may have garbage or zero strings, leading to early termination or missed comparisons. It should iterate only up to k.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes that all entries up to k are non-empty, but uninitialized entries may have garbage or zero strings, leading to early termination or missed comparisons. It should iterate only up to k.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes that all entries up to the first empty string are valid, but uninitialized entries may contain garbage or zero-length strings by chance, causing premature termination or incorrect comparisons.]

62.reason: [Same issue as above: loop condition is incorrect. Should loop from 0 to k-1, not until first empty string, because array may not be densely packed with valid entries at the beginning due to initialization state.]

83.reason: [This loop condition is wrong. After sorting, valid entries are only from 0 to k-1. The condition may stop early if a sorted entry has an empty string (which shouldn't happen), or worse, run past k if no empty string exists, causing undefined behavior. It should loop i from 0 to k.]
